exemplar.title: To what extent has Qatarâs soft power image been undermined by Western mainstream media portrayals during the 2022 FIFA World Cup?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Qatarâs soft power image been undermined by Western mainstream media portrayals during the 2022 FIFA World Cup?

strengths: Research question is explicitly stated and sharply focused, providing a clear investigative path.
Topic is communicated accurately with comprehensive explanation in the introduction and abstract.
Use of a mixed-methods design shows a generally relevant methodology with some evidence of informed source selection.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks detailed explanation of analytical procedures and critical appraisal of source bias.
Some subsections drift from the central evaluative thrust, interrupting the focus.
The integration of interview data is limitedâtranscripts are relegated to the appendix without synthesis in the analysis.
Conclusion omits reflection on methodological limitations, weakening scholarly integrity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work demonstrates a clear, well-defined research question and adequate explanation of key concepts, placing it above the lower bands. However, gaps in methodological transparency, occasional digressions from the core focus and lack of critical appraisal prevent it from reaching the 5â6 band. On balance, this warrants a mid-band score of 3 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates clear knowledge of soft power theory, realism, liberalism and human-rights frameworks.
Frequent and mostly accurate use of relevant global politics terminology (e.g., soft power, legitimacy, sports-washing).
Appropriate application of quotations, statistics and primary data to support key points.
weaknesses: Reliance on less reputable outlets (e.g., tabloid sources) and anecdotal evidence weakens authority.
Conceptual imprecision (e.g., conflating theoretical positions) and occasional factual slips reduce coherence.
Citations contain broken links and inconsistent formatting, detracting from scholarly rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows generally clear and coherent knowledge with mostly appropriate application of source material, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, weaknesses in source credibility, conceptual precision and citation consistency limit its performance to a score of 3 out of 6.

strengths: Presents a balanced, reasoned argument with acknowledgment of both negative and positive Western media portrayals.
Employs a broad research corpus (NGO reports, media outlets, academic articles, survey, interview) largely relevant to the question.
Uses case tables and theoretical contrasts (realist vs. liberalist readings) to ground analysis in evidence.
weaknesses: Critical appraisal is uneven: counter-arguments are mentioned but not deeply interrogated.
Causal mechanisms linking media narratives to soft-power outcomes are insufficiently developed.
Empirical claims (e.g., on migrant labour or infographic interpretation) lack systematic supporting data.
Interview excerpts lack contextual detail, weakening interpretive validity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs essay demonstrates good research relevance and an adequately supported, reasoned argument, consistent with the middle of the 4â6 band. However, the analysis lacks the sustained critical depth and rigorous evaluation required for higher bands, justifying a score of 4 out of 12.

strengths: Clear overall structure with a formal title page, abstract, table of contents and logical section numbering.
Sub-headings align with the analytical framework and assist reader navigation.
Primary-data appendices are correctly organized and referenced.
weaknesses: Noticeable formatting glitches (visible HTML tags, blurred images, oversized figures).
Inconsistent citation style and bibliography formatting with duplicate entries and URL errors.
Typographical errors remain (e.g., âoncludesâ in the conclusion), indicating a need for final proofreading.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the essay meets the basic requirements for structure and layout, recurring formatting and citation errors detract from its polish. This performance aligns with the lower half of the 3â4 band, warranting a score of 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student moves beyond description by contrasting realist and liberalist readings, analysing how media narratives intersect with Qatarâs policies and public perceptions. The use of survey data and case tables provides some empirical grounding.  
â¢ However, lines of reasoning sometimes rely on assertion (e.g., claims of Eurocentrism) without systematic evidence, and the causal link between media coverage and soft-power erosion is not rigorously tested.  
â¢ Conclusions are partially supported and occasionally repetitive, fitting the descriptor of âadequate analysis with partially supported conclusionsâ, so a score of 4 in the 4-6 band is appropriate.

The research corpus spans NGO reports, major Western and non-Western media, academic journals, an expert interview and an original survey, giving breadth and some depth.  
â¢ Most evidence selected bears clear relevance to assessing Western media impact on Qatarâs soft power; tables of strengths/weaknesses and explicit consideration of source bias show awareness of credibility.  
â¢ Yet the modest survey size, inclusion of a few marginal outlets and limited triangulation of primary data signal only âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ research quality, warranting a mid-band score of 5.

A formal title page, abstract, detailed table of contents and clearly numbered sections (Introduction, Methodology, Analysis, Conclusion) create a logical progression that assists reader understanding.  
â¢ Sub-headings align with the analytical framework (soft power, human rights, cultural clashes), and primary-data appendices are annexed properly.  
â¢ Some sections are overlong, and occasional repetition disrupts flow, yet overall the structure is clear and appropriate, aligning with the top band (3-4) and earning a 4.

The essay demonstrates **clear knowledge of soft-power theory, realism, liberalism, universalism vs cultural relativism, and human-rights frameworks**, applying them to the Qatari case.  
â¢ Background on Qatarâs investments, labour reforms and media environment shows a competent grasp of both empirical detail and discipline-specific debates.  
â¢ Occasional conceptual imprecision (e.g., conflating realism with Qatari officialsâ statements) and some factual slips prevent a fully âcoherentâ presentation, yet the overall command of subject matter is above adequate, meriting a 4 within the 3-4 band.

The essay presents a balanced discussion, acknowledging both negative and positive Western portrayals and evaluating realist versus liberalist perspectives. Survey findings and interview excerpts are used to gauge impact and provide some evaluative commentary.  
â¢ Yet the critical appraisal of evidence is uneven; counter-arguments are mentioned but not deeply interrogated, and the final judgement (âmoderate-to-high extentâ) is asserted rather than methodically weighed.  
â¢ This constitutes a âgood reasoned argument with some evaluationâ, but lacks the sustained critical depth of the higher bands, justifying a score of 4 within 4-6.

The essay conforms to IB layout requirements: title page, numbering, consistent font, in-text citations and a comprehensive bibliography. Visuals are captioned and placed logically.  
â¢ However, there are noticeable formatting glitches (HTML tags visible, duplicated captions, some blurred images) and several URL errors, which detract from overall polish.  
â¢ Because most but not all layout elements are correctly applied, the work meets but does not exceed expectations, meriting a 3 within the 3-4 band.

Global-politics terminology such as **soft power, sovereignty, legitimacy, globalization, sports-washing, realism, liberalism** is employed frequently and usually accurately. The student defines key terms in the methodology and applies them in analysis, aiding reader comprehension.  
â¢ There are lapsesâoccasional misuse (âlegeslativeâ, âintersectionalizationâ) and stylistic inconsistenciesâbut these do not obscure meaning.  
â¢ Because the usage is largely accurate and sustained, though not flawless, the work fits securely at the upper end of the 3-4 band with a score of 4.

The title, abstract and opening paragraphs make the focus on âWestern mainstream media portrayalsâ and âQatarâs soft-power imageâ immediately clear. Key concepts such as soft power, legitimacy and cultural diplomacy are briefly defined and situated within the 2022 World Cup context, giving readers a solid frame for what follows.  
â¢ There is a sustained effort to explain why the topic is significant in global politics (first Arab World Cup, human-rights controversies, sports-washing debates).  
â¢ Minor weaknessesâoccasional digressions and some stylistic clutterâslightly reduce overall clarity, so the strand does not fully merit the very top of the band but comfortably achieves the 5â6 level.

The student employs a mixed-methods designâliterature review, content analysis of media articles, an original survey (45 respondents) and an expert interview.  
â¢ A reasonable variety of primary (survey, interview) and secondary (academic journals, NGO reports, news outlets) sources is outlined in a methodology section that weighs strengths and weaknesses, indicating some informed selection.  
â¢ However, the surveyâs sampling limitations, occasional reliance on less scholarly sources (e.g. HOTNEWS), and a lack of explicit explanation of analytical procedures prevent this from reaching the highest band; overall the methodology is âmostly completeâ and relevant, warranting a solid 4.

The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and is framed as a clear âto what extentâ inquiry, directing attention to one soft-power variable (Western media portrayals) during a defined period (2022 World Cup).  
â¢ Throughout the essay, sections on soft power, human-rights abuses and cultural clashes return to the wording of the question, showing a strong through-line from RQ to argument.  
â¢ Some subsections (e.g. extended survey data description) drift from the central evaluative thrust, indicating the focus is strong rather than impeccably sharp; hence a mid-high score of 5.

Source material is generally appropriate (NGO reports, academic articles, major news outlets, primary data). The student integrates quotations, statistics and survey graphics to support points about labour conditions, cultural clashes and media framing.  
â¢ Nevertheless, some evidence is anecdotal or drawn from less reputable outlets, and citations occasionally contain broken links or inconsistent formatting, weakening authority.  
â¢ Application is therefore âmostly relevant and appropriateâ rather than consistently precise or critically selected, placing the work in the middle of the 3â4 band with a score of 3.

annotations.comment: The research question is explicitly stated and sharply focused, creating a clear investigative path. To strengthen it further, define key terms like âWestern mainstream mediaâ early to remove any potential ambiguity.
Footnote formatting displays inconsistent URL encoding and style. Adopt a consistent citation format (e.g., APA or Chicago) throughout to enhance professional presentation.

The strengths/weaknesses table lists basic source attributes but lacks critical appraisal of bias or comparative credibility. Deepen this evaluation by ranking sources and discussing potential reliability issues.

The reflection on survey design adjustments shows personal engagement and analytical thinking. Explicitly link these refinements back to the research question to highlight methodological decision-making.
The term âintersectionalizationâ is used without definition or citation. Replace with a precise concept or provide a sourced definition to avoid conceptual ambiguity.
Relying on TutorChase notes for theoretical framing weakens scholarly authority. Incorporate peer-reviewed academic sources when defining core concepts like hard vs. soft power.
The case table juxtaposes strengths and weaknesses but stops short of analytical linkage to soft-power outcomes. Elaborate on causal mechanisms to deepen the analysis.
Interview excerpts lack precise timestamps or context. Include exact interview dates and timestamps to improve traceability and academic rigor.
The conclusion reiterates findings but omits reflection on methodological limitations (e.g., sample size). Acknowledging these would sharpen focus and scholarly integrity.
Typographical error âoncludesâ appears in the conclusion. Conduct a final proofread to correct such mistakes and ensure clarity.
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted and not alphabetized. Apply a uniform citation style (e.g., APA) for all references to enhance presentation quality.
The narrative on migrant labour is impactful but leans toward emotional appeal. Introduce precise statistical context (e.g., injury/death rates) to ensure analytical neutrality.
The UNDHR-based critique is strong, but counter-evidence on Qatari reforms is missing. Integrate data on implemented labour laws to present a balanced evaluation.
Figure 2 is oversized and lacks a clear in-text discussion. Standardize figure dimensions and refer explicitly to its data in the surrounding analysis.
The alt-text for Figure 3 is overly verbose. Condense it to key insights in the main text and relocate full table details to an appendix to improve readability.
The discussion of Western universalism cites Jiang but fails to link the theory back to media narratives. Clarify how this critique informs Western coverage of Qatar.
Analysis mentions the BBC âLife in Qatarâ infographic but does not interpret how specific statistics shaped Western media framing. Draw explicit connections to enrich the argument.
Inclusion of tabloid sources like The Irish Sun weakens source credibility. Prioritize reputable journalism or academic analyses when illustrating positive coverage.
This section concludes abruptly without a transition to the conclusion. Add a bridging paragraph to improve structural flow between analysis and final remarks.
URLs in your bibliography vary in protocol and contain extraneous parameters. Standardize links (prefer HTTPS) and remove tracking codes for professional polish.
The full interview transcript is relegated to the appendix without integration. Summarize key insights from this qualitative data within the analysis to enhance methodological transparency.
Duplicate bibliography entries appear across pages 20â22. Remove repeated sources to streamline the reference list and avoid redundancy.
Interview excerpts lack contextual detail (e.g., interview medium, date). Provide this information to strengthen reliability and interpretive validity.
